title: What role did France play in the American Revolution?
subhead: On July 4, Americans are celebrating the 250th anniversary of their independence – an event in which the French played a part.

title: Michael Olise, France's heaven-sent prodigy

Germany sees mass protests around far-right AfD congress
subhead: The far-right AfD party's congress, held in the eastern city of Erfurt, comes ahead of September state elections, in which the party could win one or two eastern states. The event coincides with the 100th anniversary of a Nazi Party congress.

title: 'Clipification': How short-form videos are reshaping the media landscape
subhead: While not a new concept, clips have become, over just a few years, the dominant form of expression on the internet, extending far beyond advertising. The cultural and cognitive effects of this format are now a subject of debate.

title: Paul Seixas, the 19-year-old phenom carrying France's Tour hopes
subhead: For his first appearance at the Tour de France, which begins Saturday in Barcelona, the 19-year-old French cyclist is already seen as one of the top rivals to four-time winner Tadej Pogacar.

title: China's bridge and highway construction boom runs up against wall of debt in Guizhou
subhead: A symbol of China's ambitious infrastructure policy, the southern province of Guizhou has seen mega-bridges, airports and new neighborhoods proliferate. This excessive surge has also resulted in unmanageable government debt, corruption scandals and unfinished construction projects.

title: Early heatwaves are devastating French agriculture and leaving farmers helpless
subhead: Scorched crops, decimated livestock and double-digit production drops: as farmers take stock of the effects of extreme heat, they fear the worst is yet to come.

title: The Christian right's charge to rewrite American history
subhead: 'America 250' (12/13). Barack Obama's election in 2008 led to greater recognition of the role of minorities in nation-building. But the conservative backlash soon followed. Leading the 'patriotic' counter-offensive, Donald Trump has received support from the influential Hillsdale College.
